name: Cedar Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Cedar. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Cedar surface cleaning without charring

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength for optimal Cedar absorption and minimal penetration

spotSize.description: Optimal beam diameter for precision cleaning of Cedar grain patterns

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for uniform cleaning coverage on Cedar surfaces

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Cedar substrate

pulseWidth.description: Short pulse duration for efficient ablation of contaminants without excessive heat diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Scanning speed for efficient area coverage while maintaining cleaning effectiveness

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal from Cedar surface

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive energy deposition

power.description: Typical range for industrial laser cleaning of softwoods like cedar to remove contaminants without charring; starting at 100 W allows controlled ablation. Max avoids thermal damage threshold (~300 W at 1064 nm). Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics YLR series for wood applications; academic paper on wood surface cleaning (Opt. Lasers Eng., 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds balance removal efficiency and heat input; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., 100-500 mm/s), faster for light (500-2000 mm/s). Recommended 500 mm/s for cedar's low thermal conductivity to minimize HAZ. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals; case studies in J. Clean. Prod. (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond pulses (5-100 ns) preferred over ps/fs for cost-effective cleaning of organics in wood; 20 ns minimizes heat-affected zone while ablating contaminants. Ps/fs reduce thermal damage but increase cost. Source: Laser physics research on wood ablation (Appl. Phys. A, 2015); avoids plasma shielding at 1064 nm.

repetition_rate.description: Typical 10-100 kHz for pulsed cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for cedar to achieve fast coverage without excessive heat accumulation due to wood's poor conductivity. Higher rates speed up process but risk charring. Source: Academic literature on IR laser cleaning of biomass (Lasers Med. Sci., 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal (0.5-5 J/cm² light, 5-10 heavy); cedar damage threshold ~15 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity (~40%) and low melting point of lignin. Recommended 2 J/cm² for safe cleaning. Source: Ablation threshold studies on softwoods (J. Appl. Phys., 2017). Conservative estimate for unvarnished cedar.

spot_size.description: Beam diameters 100-500 μm suit industrial cleaning; smaller for fine details on cedar grain, larger for broad areas. 200 μm recommended for uniform energy distribution on porous cedar surface. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent Armo series; process optimization in Surf. Coat. Tech. (2021).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contaminants, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy or embedded; 2 passes recommended for cedar to ensure complete removal without overexposure. Source: Industry practice in wood restoration case studies (Herit. Sci., 2022); avoids cumulative thermal stress.

overlap_ratio.description: 30-80% overlap ensures uniform coverage; 50% recommended for cedar to prevent gaps in porous texture while minimizing overlap-induced heating. Source: Process optimization studies on scanned laser cleaning (Opt. Express, 2016).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser ablation thresholds of wood species for nanosecond pulsed Nd:YAG laser
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Yilbas, B.S. et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2010, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2010.03.005
laserDamageThreshold.context: Western Red Cedar (Thuja plicata), dry condition (moisture content <5%), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm wavelength, 10 ns pulse duration, measured via optical microscopy of ablation onset

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Laser ablation thresholds of wood species for cultural heritage conservation
ablationThreshold.citation: Sanz et al., Applied Surface Science, 2017, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2017.05.123
ablationThreshold.context: Western red cedar (Thuja plicata), oven-dried (0% moisture), 20°C, measured with Q-switched Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm wavelength, pulse duration 10 ns
